pi_items.source: Task scripts (setup_task.sh, verifier.py) confirm the requirements to add watermarks, burn timecodes, process batches of videos, extract thumbnails, and write metadata catalogs.
Task description and scripts require batch transcoding of mock stock footage with watermarks.
O*NET / BLS SOC definitions do not map 15-1299 to "Media Operations Specialist". It maps to "Computer Occupations, All Other" and its related sub-codes.
NAICS taxonomy does not have an industry officially named "Stock Photography and Footage" (it falls under broader codes like 541922 or 519130).

privileged_info_summary: The task relies on synthetically generated mock video footage created locally via ffmpeg filters (mandelbrot, life, testsrc2). The agent must transcode these into 1280x720 H.264 preview videos approximately 15 seconds in duration, with a visible text watermark and a burned-in timecode overlay. The agent must also extract thumbnails at >=640px width and compile a catalog.json file detailing all source and output metadata. Verification leverages both local file statistics and a VLM to confirm the visual presence of the watermark and timecode overlays.
